sections.heading: Professional summary
points: Jeremiah Gage is a software engineer and front-end technical lead based in Missouri.
His public portfolio in this repository emphasizes professional software delivery, leadership, and clear communication.
He has professional experience across e-commerce, healthcare software, and higher-education support roles.

sections.heading: Professional experience
points: At O'Reilly Auto Parts in Springfield, Missouri and remote, Jeremiah has held multiple roles including UI/UX Developer II beginning in February 2022, Technical Lead beginning in March 2024, Scrum Master in a part-time capacity beginning in June 2025, Software Engineer I from June 2025 through September 2025, and Software Engineer II beginning in September 2025.

The O'Reilly portfolio content describes work across the full software development life cycle, front-end technical leadership, cloud-based solutions, code reviews, process improvement, team collaboration, mentoring, and Scrum facilitation.
Before O'Reilly, Jeremiah worked at Netsmart in Springfield, Missouri as a Software Engineer from July 2020 through February 2022.
The approved portfolio data describes Netsmart work across software design, development, review, unit and integration testing, deployment, and technical solutions for business requirements.

Earlier, Jeremiah worked at College of the Ozarks as a part-time Lab Assistant from September 2017 through June 2020 in the mathematics and physics department, supporting students in subjects such as calculus, calculus-based physics, abstract algebra, statistics, numerical analysis, and real analysis.

sections.heading: Technical skills
points: Technologies associated with O'Reilly roles in the approved portfolio data include Vue, React, TypeScript, Node.js, Thymeleaf, Docker, Google Cloud Platform, jQuery, Google Tag Manager, and semantic HTML/CSS.
Technologies associated with the Netsmart role include DynamoDB, React, TypeScript, Telerik/Kendo UI, .NET Core, Node.js, Go, HTML, and jQuery.

sections.heading: Communication and work style signals
points: The public portfolio content consistently frames Jeremiah as someone who values mentorship, collaboration, documentation, testing, and delivery discipline.
The approved data shows repeated involvement in leadership, teaching, tutoring, and Scrum facilitation, which suggests a communication-oriented engineering style.
If asked about information not covered here, direct the visitor to contact Jeremiah rather than guessing.
